scope: Artifact-only apply approval packet for future local remediation of IF09-FIND-001 S3.
candidate_change_surface: Canonical sync packet assembly logic responsible for selecting and persisting latest_completed_project_commit_sha.
Primary-ref verification logic that compares the recorded project SHA against the verified canonical ref before packet acceptance.
Drift-artifact emission or fail-closed handling for SHA mismatch conditions.
Regression coverage proving canonical SHA sync behavior and forbidden-surface preservation.
future_files_or_modules_explicitly_out_o: candidate or invalid findings outside IF09-FIND-001
preconditions_for_future_apply: A future apply request must name the exact canonical sync control to be changed before any code edit or patch creation is authorized.
A deterministic verified-primary-ref selection rule must be documented and reviewable before future apply.
Future apply must specify drift-artifact emission or fail-closed behavior for SHA mismatch before execution begins.
Regression coverage for IF10-REG-001 at tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py must be ready before future apply.
All real execution, runtime, secrets, product, Bedrock, revalidation, and finding-close locks must remain false unless a later explicit operator command changes them.
future_apply_authorization_checklist: Exact files/modules to be touched are named and remain within the candidate change surface.
The future diff is bounded to canonical SHA verification hardening and does not expand into runtime or product scope.
A command/test plan is prepared for tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py and validator rerun after the future apply.
Before/after evidence capture steps are defined for stale-SHA state, corrected SHA state, and drift-artifact or fail-closed behavior.
Rollback steps are documented and feasible without structural schema drift.
An explicit operator command exists for local remediation apply; without it, apply remains prohibited.
required_tests_after_future_apply: Run tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py with assertions for verified-primary-ref equality and mismatch handling.
Run validator flow to confirm post-apply artifacts preserve forbidden execution surfaces as false.
Run any targeted canonical sync regression checks needed to prove latest_completed_project_commit_sha is no longer retained from stale packet metadata.
required_before_after_evidence_after_fut: Before evidence showing the stale-SHA condition recorded for IF09-FIND-001.
After evidence showing the corrected canonical packet derives SHA from verified primary refs.
Evidence that mismatch custody is preserved by explicit drift artifact or fail-closed path after apply.
A no-real-execution / no-real-apply attestation for the proof gate surrounding the future apply.
rollback_requirements_for_future_apply: Rollback must restore the prior canonical sync control if post-apply regression coverage fails.
Rollback must preserve emitted drift artifacts and must not erase custody evidence.
Rollback must leave validator pass, or the future apply gate must terminate as blocked rather than claiming success.
blast_radius_limits_for_future_apply: Future apply is limited to active_context_governance and canonical sync packet verification surfaces.
No runtime mutation, product behavior, Bedrock readiness, secrets, external network, MCP, RAG, memory-write, or real_apply surfaces may be touched.
No unrelated findings, candidate findings, or invalid findings may be promoted or modified.
apply_blocking_conditions: The future apply request does not include an explicit operator command.
The candidate change surface cannot be mapped to concrete files/modules within canonical sync handling.
Regression tests or assertions for IF10-REG-001 remain undefined, ambiguous, or unavailable.
The future apply would require structural schema drift outside approved scope.
The proposed change still depends on silent SHA normalization or any lock inversion not separately approved.
